message:
security(dist): SHA-pin cosign-installer in release workflow

The release signs every artifact with sigstore cosign before publishing.
The cosign-installer step was pinned only to the `@v4.0.0` tag. A
compromised tag push (or GitHub Actions cache/mirror tampering) could
substitute a malicious installer into the signing step, undermining the
entire integrity chain built by the subsequent `cosign sign-blob` calls
and the SHA256SUMS / sigstore-bundle fan-out consumed by install.sh.

Pin to the immutable commit SHA `faadad0cce49287aee09b3a48701e75088a2c6ad`
(the v4.0.0 commit as reported by `gh api repos/sigstore/cosign-installer/
commits/v4.0.0`) with a trailing `# v4.0.0` comment so future Dependabot
runs keep the human-readable version context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -215,7 +215,11 @@ jobs:
         run: ls -laR dist
 
       - name: Install cosign
-        uses: sigstore/cosign-installer@v4.0.0
+        # SHA-pinned to the v4.0.0 commit so a tag re-push (malicious or
+        # accidental) cannot substitute a compromised action into the
+        # signing step — the entire sigstore integrity chain hinges on this
+        # action's code being the one we audited.
+        uses: sigstore/cosign-installer@faadad0cce49287aee09b3a48701e75088a2c6ad # v4.0.0
 
       - name: Sign release artifacts (sigstore bundle)
         run: |
